claim: The list of available tools shall be generated from a single methodology.Tools registry (internal/methodology), never hand-maintained in prose -- each tool carries its Canon section, purpose, and Ported/Declared status, and the EMBEDDED-TOOLS block of CLAUDE.md is rendered from it.
why: The docs-as-code pattern applied to tool inventories -- a new tool without a registry entry simply does not appear in the tool reference, making drift structurally visible. In the Go port the registry is methodology.Tools (internal/methodology/tools_data.go), populated via Tools.MustRegister in an init() block: each entry carries Command, Canon (§-section), Purpose (the usage/claim text), and Status (Ported or Declared). The EMBEDDED-TOOLS block in CLAUDE.md is rendered from this registry (internal/generator), so the tool reference on every gen-spec is always in sync with the declared tool set. A separate data table (internal/generator/tool_reqs.go: toolRequirementData) projects each tool into a STRUCTURAL R-tool-<basename> requirement (R-tool-is-its-own-requirement). The historical Python mechanism scanned spec/tools/*.py and per-agent spec/agents/<name>/tools/*.py on the filesystem; in the Go port the filesystem scan was replaced by the explicit registry (tools are Go CLI subcommands, not discoverable .py modules), but the 'generated, never hand-maintained in prose' discipline is preserved: both the registry and the tool_reqs table are code, and the rendered output is always derived from them. PROSE (not ENFORCED): the registry-completeness property has no enforcer test in the Go port (the historical tests were test_repo_map.py::test_repo_map_complete and test_repo_map_tool_xref_present -- Python-only).
